title: diagnostics_channel: fix race condition with diagnostics_channel and GC
category: FinalizationRegistry race (a late finalizer deletes a freshly re-created entry)
crash_site: Not a crash — a silent correctness bug: a diagnostics_channel subscription/entry is lost after GC, so published messages stop reaching subscribers (or a stale entry is observed). Surfaces as 'subscriber intermittently stops receiving events' after garbage collection, not a stack trace.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: Channel / ActiveChannel publish/subscribe/unsubscribe (they look like where a subscriber is lost, but are correct); the WeakRef construction. The bug is the WeakRefMap FinalizationRegistry #finalizers callback deleting unconditionally.
ground_truth.fix_summary: WeakRefMap's FinalizationRegistry callback (#finalizers) did this.delete(key) unconditionally. Finalizers run asynchronously after GC, so if a new WeakRef replaced the old one for the same key BEFORE the old ref's finalizer fired, the finalizer deleted the FRESH entry -> the channel/subscriber map silently loses a live entry (race between GC finalization and re-creation). Fix: the finalizer now deletes only if the entry is truly gone (`if (!this.has(key)) this.delete(key)`), plus a new has() override that checks `!!this.get(key)` (a live value) rather than SafeMap.has().

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD + no crash: a GC-timing race where a late finalizer removes a re-created map entry, silently dropping a diagnostics subscription. Live decoy = the subscribe/publish path. Authored from PR #59910 fix diff (WeakRefMap #finalizers guards on !has(key); new has() override).

provenance: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/59910 — diagnostics_channel: fix race with diagnostics_channel and GC (merged 2025-09-19); fix commit 897932c4848a; base c7b0dfbd7c56. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (WeakRefMap #finalizers guard + has() override).
